1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   138→
   139→```
   140→atomic/atoms/Button/
   141→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143→└── Models/
   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```
   146→
   147→**Models/index.d.ts**
   148→
   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {
   151→  text: string;
   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {
   156→  pressed: boolean;
   157→}
   158→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159→  handlePress: () => void;
   160→}
   161→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162→```
   163→
   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```
   185→
   186→**view.tsx (render)**
   187→
   188→```tsx
   189→import React from 'react';
   190→import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191→import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192→import { useButton } from './index';
   193→import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194→
   195→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197→
   198→  return (
   199→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200→      <View>
   201→        <Text>
   202→          {props.text}
   203→          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
   204→        </Text>
   205→      </View>
   206→    </Pressable>
   207→  );
   208→}
   209→```
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Project Organization
   214→
   215→```
   216→src/
   217→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   218→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220→│   ├── atoms/
   221→│   ├── molecules/
   222→│   └── organisms/
   223→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   228→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   231→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   236→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   238→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240→```
   241→
   242→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```
   325→
   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```
   370→
   371→- For Use on Class.
   372→
   373→```ts
   374→class Auth {
   375→  private readonly setAuth;
   376→
   377→  constructor () {
   378→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  function doSomething(data) {
   382→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→```
   386→
   387→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   389→
   390→```ts
   391→const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392→const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393→```
   394→
   395→---
   396→
   397→## Routes Naming
   398→
   399→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   400→
   401→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   402→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   403→
   404→---
   405→
   406→## Folder Naming Rules
   407→
   408→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
   409→| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
   410→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
   411→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
   412→
   413→---
   414→
   415→## Code Standards
   416→
   417→**Language**: English only.  
   418→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   419→
   420→**Explanatory variables**
   421→
   422→```js
   423→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   424→const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
   425→  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   426→const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
   427→isNumber(1);
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Semantic constants**
   431→
   432→```js
   433→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   434→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   435→```
   436→
   437→**Library workarounds with link**
   438→
   439→```js
   440→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   441→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   442→event.target.value = '';
   443→```
   444→
   445→**JSDoc**
   446→
   447→```js
   448→/**
   449→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   450→ * @param {*} eventName
   451→ * @param {*} maxLength
   452→ */
   453→```
   454→
   455→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→useEffect(() => {
   459→  (async () => {
   460→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   461→    setSum(42);
   462→  })();
   463→}, [setSum]);
   464→```
   465→
   466→**Extract conditions**
   467→
   468→```ts
   469→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   470→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   471→```
   472→
   473→**Optional chaining**
   474→
   475→```ts
   476→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   477→```
   478→
   479→**JSX indentation**
   480→
   481→```tsx
   482→{
   483→  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
   484→}
   485→```
   486→
   487→**Early returns**
   488→
   489→```ts
   490→if (!data) return null;
   491→return <View />;
   492→```
   493→
   494→**Nullish coalescing**
   495→
   496→```ts
   497→return data ?? 0;
   498→```
   499→
   500→---
   501→
   502→## Styles Patterns
   503→
   504→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   505→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   506→
   507→---
   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   557→
   558→---
   559→
   560→## Checklist
   561→
   562→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   563→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   564→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   565→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   566→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   567→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   568→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   569→
   570→---
   571→
   572→Happy shipping! 🚀
   573→

1→
     2→Proposta: Server Driven UI (SDUI): Um Padrão para Telas Parametrizáveis
     3→Status: Rascunho
     4→Versão: 1.0
     5→
     6→
     7→                              Resumo
     8→
     9→   Esta proposta descreve uma forma de estruturar o conteúdo das
    10→   telas parametrizáveis (vouchers, benefícios, resgates, etc)
    11→   de forma padronizada para reduzir a necessidade de deploy quando
    12→   há mudanças de texto, formatação ou ordem de elementos.
    13→
    14→   A proposta é baseada em padrões utilizados por empresas como
    15→   Airbnb, Nubank e DoorDash, adaptados para nossa realidade.
    16→
    17→
    18→Índice
    19→
    20→   1. Contexto
    21→   2. Proposta
    22→   3. Como Funciona
    23→      3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    24→      3.2. Componentes
    25→      3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    26→      3.4. Valores e Contexto
    27→      3.5. Regras e Condicionais
    28→   4. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
    29→   5. Implementação
    30→   6. Referências
    31→
    32→
    33→1. Contexto
    34→
    35→   Muitas aplicações possuem telas parametrizáveis, funcionando como 
    36→   templates, que recebem dados do backend, definidos conforme a 
    37→   necessidade de cada template. Na prática, os valores são dinâmicos,
    38→   mas a estrutura não. A cada nova necessidade de alteração simples de
    39→   layout e formatação, como destacar um texto em negrito, um novo campo é
    40→   adicionado no JSON de resposta do servidor e uma nova regra condicional
    41→   (if) é adicionada no frontend, resultando em armazenamento de dados legados
    42→   e no acúmulo de código morto.
    43→
    44→2. Proposta: Padrão SDUI.
    45→    
    46→    É um padrão arquitetural no qual o backend define a lógica de exibição 
    47→    dos dados, indo além da definição de parâmetros textuais.
    48→
    49→    Empresas que usam esse padrão:
    50→
    51→        - Nubank: 70% das novas telas usam esse formato
    52→        - Airbnb: Busca, listagens e checkout
    53→        - DoorDash: Reduziu tempo de mudanças de semanas para horas
    54→
    55→3. Como Funciona
    56→
    57→3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    58→
    59→     Uma tela é composta por:
    60→
    61→     {
    62→       "screen": {
    63→         "id": "voucher_form",
    64→         "context": { ... },      // dados para usar nos componentes
    65→         "header": { ... },       // cabeçalho
    66→         "body": [ ... ],         // array de componentes
    67→         "footer": { ... }        // rodapé (botão de ação)
    68→       }
    69→     }
    70→
    71→     O body é um array. A ordem do array é a ordem na tela.
    72→
    73→3.2. Componentes
    74→
    75→     Cada componente tem um tipo e propriedades:
    76→
    77→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Título" } }
    78→     { "type": "input", "props": { "label": "Valor em pontos" } }
    79→     { "type": "button", "props": { "label": "Continuar" } }
    80→
    81→     O app tem um registro de componentes:
    82→
    83→     const Componentes = {
    84→       'heading': HeadingComponent,
    85→       'input': InputComponent,
    86→       'button': ButtonComponent,
    87→       'balance': BalanceComponent,
    88→       // ...
    89→     };
    90→
    91→     Quando recebe o JSON, renderiza cada item do array usando
    92→     o componente correspondente ao tipo.
    93→
    94→     Tipos que já temos no Design System podem ser mapeados
    95→     diretamente. Não precisa criar componentes novos.
    96→
    97→3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    98→
    99→     Para textos que precisam de formatação inline (negrito,
   100→     links, ícones), usamos uma estrutura de "nodes":
   101→
   102→     {
   103→       "type": "rich_text",
   104→       "children": [
   105→         { "type": "text", "value": "Confira os " },
   106→         { "type": "text", "value": "termos", "bold": true },
   107→         { "type": "text", "value": " ou " },
   108→         { "type": "link", "text": "fale conosco", "to": "app://suporte" }
   109→       ]
   110→     }
   111→
   112→     Por que não markdown?
   113→
   114→     Se usarmos [texto](url) para links e depois precisarmos
   115→     adicionar tracking ou confirmação, temos que refatorar o
   116→     parser.
   117→
   118→     Com nodes, é só adicionar uma propriedade:
   119→
   120→     {
   121→       "type": "link",
   122→       "text": "fale conosco",
   123→       "to": "app://suporte",
   124→       "trackEvent": "suporte_click"    // adicionado depois
   125→     }
   126→
   127→3.4. Valores e Contexto
   128→
   129→
   130→     O Backend envia os valores no campo context:
   131→     {
   132→       "context": {
   133→         "balance": {
   134→           "points": 25720,
   135→           "value": 7716.00
   136→         }
   137→       }
   138→     }
   139→
   140→
   141→     Para textos que dependem do contexto, usamos interpolação:
   142→
   143→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Voucher para {{product.name}}" } }
   144→
   145→     O app substitui {{product.name}} pelo valor do contexto.
   146→
   147→3.5. Regras e Condicionais
   148→
   149→     Para controlar visibilidade e habilitação, usamos regras
   150→     simples baseadas em JSON Logic:
   151→
   152→     Botão habilitado só quando valor > 0:
   153→
   154→     {
   155→       "type": "button",
   156→       "props": {
   157→         "label": "Continuar",
   158→         "enabled": {
   159→           "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   160→         }
   161→       }
   162→     }
   163→
   164→     Os operadores suportados são básicos:
   165→     - var (acesso a dados)
   166→     - ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparação)
   167→     - and, or, ! (lógica)
   168→
   169→     Não tem loops, não tem eval, não tem risco.
   170→
   171→     Validação de inputs segue o mesmo padrão:
   172→
   173→     {
   174→       "type": "input",
   175→       "props": {
   176→         "id": "points",
   177→         "validation": {
   178→           "rules": [
   179→             {
   180→               "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "value" }, 0] },
   181→               "message": "Informe um valor maior que zero"
   182→             }
   183→           ]
   184→         }
   185→       }
   186→     }
   187→
   188→5. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
   189→
   190→   Estrutura completa para a tela de voucher:
   191→
   192→   {
   193→     "screen": {
   194→       "id": "voucher_form",
   195→       "context": {
   196→         "product": {
   197→           "key": "abastecimento",
   198→           "name": "Abastecimento"
   199→         },
   200→         "balance": {
   201→           "points": 25720,
   202→           "pointsFormatted": "25.720",
   203→           "valueFormatted": "R$ 7.716,00"
   204→         }
   205→       },
   206→       "header": {
   207→         "type": "nav_header",
   208→         "props": {
   209→           "title": "Voucher para {{product.name}}",
   210→           "showBack": true
   211→         }
   212→       },
   213→       "body": [
   214→         {
   215→           "type": "heading",
   216→           "props": { "text": "Defina o desconto para seu {{product.key}}" }
   217→         },
   218→         {
   219→           "type": "balance_display",
   220→           "props": {
   221→             "label": "Pontos disponíveis",
   222→             "points": "{{balance.pointsFormatted}}",
   223→             "value": "{{balance.valueFormatted}}"
   224→           }
   225→         },
   226→         {
   227→           "type": "points_input",
   228→           "props": {
   229→             "id": "points",
   230→             "label": "Valor em pontos",
   231→             "type": "numeric"
   232→           }
   233→         },
   234→         {
   235→           "type": "points_input",
   236→           "props": {
   237→             "id": "reais",
   238→             "label": "Valor em reais",
   239→             "type": "currency"
   240→           }
   241→         },
   242→         {
   243→           "type": "link",
   244→           "props": {
   245→             "text": "Exibir regras de uso",
   246→             "to": "app://voucher/rules"
   247→           }
   248→         }
   249→       ],
   250→       "footer": {
   251→         "type": "button",
   252→         "props": {
   253→           "label": "Continuar",
   254→           "style": "primary",
   255→           "enabled": {
   256→             "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   257→           }
   258→         }
   259→       }
   260→     }
   261→   }
   262→
   263→   O mesmo código renderiza Abastecimento, Estacionamento,
   264→   Seguro Auto e IPVA. Mudando apenas o context, não o app.
   265→
   266→
   267→6. Implementação
   268→
   269→   6.1. O que precisa ser feito
   270→
   271→        - Registro de componentes (mapeamento tipo ‣ componente)
   272→        - Renderizador que itera o array e renderiza cada tipo
   273→        - Parser de interpolação (substituir {{var}})
   274→        - Avaliador de regras (subset de JSON Logic)
   275→
   276→   6.2. O que não muda
   277→
   278→        - Design System continua o mesmo
   279→        - Componentes existentes são reutilizados
   280→        - Telas atuais continuam funcionando
   281→        - Backend continua sendo Lambda que recebe/devolve JSON
   282→
   283→   6.3. Escopo inicial
   284→
   285→        Implementar na tela de Voucher (SPMAIS-34018) que já está
   286→        com feature toggle. É o cenário ideal:
   287→
   288→        - Feature nova, sem legado
   289→        - 4 variações do mesmo fluxo
   290→        - Retrabalho previsível (produto vai pedir ajustes)
   291→
   292→   6.4. Validação
   293→
   294→        O JSON pode ser validado antes de salvar no backend e
   295→        antes de renderizar no app. Componentes desconhecidos
   296→        usam fallback. Regras inválidas retornam false.
   297→
   298→7. Referências
   299→
   300→   [AIRBNB]    "A Deep Dive into Airbnb's Server-Driven UI System"
   301→               https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/a-deep-dive-into-airbnbs-server-driven-ui-system-842244c5f5
   302→
   303→   [NUBANK]    "Backend Driven Content at Nubank"
   304→               https://building.nubank.com/server-driven-ui-framework-at-nubank/
   305→
   306→   [JSONLOGIC] JSON Logic - Regras em JSON
   307→               https://jsonlogic.com/
   308→
   309→

content.text:
Here's a summary of the files in `.claude/`:

**1. `CLAUDE.md`** — Empty file (0 bytes).

**2. `STYLE_GUIDE.md`** — Frontend Style Guide for Sem Parar with:
- **Hook Component pattern**: screens split into logic hook (`index.ts`) + render view (`view.tsx`) + types (`Models/index.d.ts`)
- **Zustand-only** state management (slices under `/services/<domain>/store/`)
- **Services architecture** following SOLID: `UI → services → repository → api`
- **Atomic Design** structure (atoms/molecules/organisms)
- Cache via `GenericStorage<T>`, no `StyleSheet`/`styled-components`, use `@semparar/design-system-app`

**3. `rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt`** — RFC proposing **Server Driven UI (SDUI)**:
- Backend defines screen structure via JSON (`header`, `body[]`, `footer`)
- Component registry maps `type` → React Native component
- Rich text via `children` nodes (not markdown)
- Context interpolation with `{{var}}` syntax
- Conditional rules via JSON Logic subset
- Initial scope: Voucher screen (SPMAIS-34018)

**4. `TASK.pdf`** — Jira ticket **SPMAIS-34653** (Spike):
- Define the parameterization structure for voucher redemption screens
- Reuse the same screens built for **cashback na fatura**, dynamically updating per product (Abastecimento, Estacionamento, Seguro Auto, IPVA)
- Screens to parameterize: **Form** (define points/value), **Review** (confirm), **Conclusion** (success), **Onboarding** (first-time carousel), **Regras de uso** (rules)
- Replace the `?` tooltip (cashback) with an "Exibir regras de uso" link (voucher)
- Must use the existing `update-parameter` API
- Texts must be separated by product

What would you like me to do with this? Should I explore the codebase and plan the parameterization structure for the voucher screens?

content.thinking:
The user is asking three questions:
1. Is this project part of a micro frontend?
2. Where is the main app?
3. Is it possible to configure Storybook in this project to view screens independently from the main app?

Let me explore the codebase to understand the project structure, check if it's a micro frontend, and assess Storybook feasibility.

content.text: Let me explore the project to answer your questions.

input.description: Explore project structure and MFE setup
input.prompt:
Explore this React Native project thoroughly to answer:

1. **Is this a micro frontend (MFE)?** Look for:
   - package.json (name, dependencies, re-pack/module-federation configs)
   - Any federation or re-pack config files (webpack, rspack, etc.)
   - How modules are exported (index.ts at root, App.tsx, etc.)
   - References to a "super app" or "host app"
   - Any `@semparar/` scoped packages in dependencies

2. **Where is the main/host app?** Look for:
   - References in package.json, README, or config files to a host/shell app
   - Navigation setup (routes/) that might reveal how this MFE is loaded
   - Any federation remote/host configuration

3. **Storybook feasibility:** Look for:
   - Any existing Storybook config (.storybook/ folder)
   - Existing stories files (*.stories.tsx)
   - The design system used (@semparar/design-system-app) and how components are imported
   - React Native version and compatibility considerations
   - Any ThemeProvider or context providers that screens depend on

Search broadly: package.json, tsconfig, babel.config, metro.config, webpack/rspack configs, app.json, src/ structure, routes/, etc.

Explore this React Native project thoroughly to answer:

1. **Is this a micro frontend (MFE)?** Look for:
   - package.json (name, dependencies, re-pack/module-federation configs)
   - Any federation or re-pack config files (webpack, rspack, etc.)
   - How modules are exported (index.ts at root, App.tsx, etc.)
   - References to a "super app" or "host app"
   - Any `@semparar/` scoped packages in dependencies

2. **Where is the main/host app?** Look for:
   - References in package.json, README, or config files to a host/shell app
   - Navigation setup (routes/) that might reveal how this MFE is loaded
   - Any federation remote/host configuration

3. **Storybook feasibility:** Look for:
   - Any existing Storybook config (.storybook/ folder)
   - Existing stories files (*.stories.tsx)
   - The design system used (@semparar/design-system-app) and how components are imported
   - React Native version and compatibility considerations
   - Any ThemeProvider or context providers that screens depend on

Search broadly: package.json, tsconfig, babel.config, metro.config, webpack/rspack configs, app.json, src/ structure, routes/, etc.

3. **How are screens rendered?** Look at the routes/ folder and how navigation works.

Be thorough - check the actual file contents, not just file names.

content.content:
1→# Fintech Rewards Program
     2→
     3→Este micro-frontend é um da vertical de serviços financeiros do SuperApp do Sem Parar.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Scripts NPM
     8→
     9→- `test` - executa Jest
    10→- `test:update-snapshot` - atualiza snapshots do Jest
    11→- `test:coverage` - executa Jest com cobertura
    12→- `sync:yalc` - publica com `yalc` e faz push em mudanças para desenvolvimento integrado com o `../superAppV3`
    13→- `knip` - knip busca código e dependências sem uso ou faltando, imports errados, entre outros - [saiba mais](https://knip.dev/).
    14→- `lint` - executa checagens e formatação do `eslint`, `prettier` e `typescript`.
    15→   O comando `run-s` utilizado pelo script `lint` faz parte do [npm-run-all](https://www.npmjs.com/package/npm-run-all)
    16→
    17→## Pré-requisitos
    18→
    19→Instale as ferramentas abaixo nas versões recomendadas para garantir compatibilidade:
    20→
    21→| Ferramenta         | Versão                        | Como instalar                                            |
    22→| :----------------- | :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------- |
    23→| OpenJDK            | 17                            | `brew install --cask zulu@17`                            |
    24→| Node.js            | LTS                           | Recomendado via NVM                                      |
    25→| Ruby               | 2.7.4                         | Use rbenv/rvm                                            |
    26→| CocoaPods          | >= 1.11.2                     | `gem install cocoapods -v "~> 1.11"` ou `bundle install` |
    27→| Xcode              | Compatível com a versão do RN | Veja versões disponíveis em https://xcodereleases.com    |
    28→| Android Studio/SDK | Atual                         | Siga guia oficial do RN                                  |
    29→
    30→Notas:
    31→
    32→1. Em caso de problemas com Xcode, baixe a versão recomendada em [xcodereleases.com](https://xcodereleases.com/).
    33→2. Siga o guia oficial de ambiente iOS/Android: https://reactnative.dev/docs/set-up-your-environment
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Acesso e configuração
    38→
    39→Importante: é necessário estar conectado à VPN e logado com o usuário GitHub do Sem Parar para acesso aos repositórios.
    40→
    41→### 1) Clonar o repositório
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→git clone https://github.com/SemParar-B2C/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram.git
    45→cd rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram
    46→```
    47→
    48→### 2) Instalar dependências
    49→
    50→```bash
    51→# Node deps
    52→npm install
    53→```
    54→
    55→### 3) SuperApp (integração local)
    56→
    57→Este pacote é um micro-frontend consumido pelo SuperApp. Para desenvolver integrado:
    58→
    59→1. Tenha o SuperApp clonado como diretório `../appSemPararv3`
    60→2. Sincronize este pacote com o SuperApp:
    61→   - `npm run sync:yalc`
    62→   - Requer `yalc` e `nodemon`; publica no yalc e faz push on change.
    63→
    64→---
    65→
    66→## Consumo de código do SuperApp
    67→
    68→Este pacote é um micro-frontend usado pelo SuperApp, e aqui também importamos código do SuperApp por meio de aliases.
    69→Todo import cujo path inicia com `~/` é resolvido para o código do próprio SuperApp.
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Testes
    74→
    75→- Framework: Jest (`jest.config.js`)
    76→- Bibliotecas: `@testing-library/react-native`, `@testing-library/jest-native`
    77→- Ambiente: `testEnvironment: 'node'` com mocks RN
    78→- Paths de testes: `__tests__/**/*.test.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}`
    79→- Cobertura: saída em `__tests__/coverage` (relatórios: text, lcov, html)
    80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→## Links importantes
    84→
    85→- SharePoint Fintech: https://fleetcor.sharepoint.com/sites/SemPararMais-Fintech
    86→

Explore this React Native project thoroughly to answer:

1. **Is this a micro frontend (MFE)?** Look for:
   - package.json (name, dependencies, re-pack/module-federation configs)
   - Any federation or re-pack config files (webpack, rspack, etc.)
   - How modules are exported (index.ts at root, App.tsx, etc.)
   - References to a "super app" or "host app"
   - Any `@semparar/` scoped packages in dependencies

2. **Where is the main/host app?** Look for:
   - References in package.json, README, or config files to a host/shell app
   - Navigation setup (routes/) that might reveal how this MFE is loaded
   - Any federation remote/host configuration

3. **Storybook feasibility:** Look for:
   - Any existing Storybook config (.storybook/ folder)
   - Existing stories files (*.stories.tsx)
   - The design system used (@semparar/design-system-app) and how components are imported
   - React Native version and compatibility considerations
   - Any ThemeProvider or context providers that screens depend on

Search broadly: package.json, tsconfig, babel.config, metro.config, webpack/rspack configs, app.json, src/ structure, routes/, etc.

    27→          '^~(.+)': './src/\\1',
    28→        },
    29→      },

data.prompt:
Explore this React Native project thoroughly to answer:

1. **Is this a micro frontend (MFE)?** Look for:
   - package.json (name, dependencies, re-pack/module-federation configs)
   - Any federation or re-pack config files (webpack, rspack, etc.)
   - How modules are exported (index.ts at root, App.tsx, etc.)
   - References to a "super app" or "host app"
   - Any `@semparar/` scoped packages in dependencies

2. **Where is the main/host app?** Look for:
   - References in package.json, README, or config files to a host/shell app
   - Navigation setup (routes/) that might reveal how this MFE is loaded
   - Any federation remote/host configuration

3. **Storybook feasibility:** Look for:
   - Any existing Storybook config (.storybook/ folder)
   - Existing stories files (*.stories.tsx)
   - The design system used (@semparar/design-system-app) and how components are imported
   - React Native version and compatibility considerations
   - Any ThemeProvider or context providers that screens depend on

Search broadly: package.json, tsconfig, babel.config, metro.config, webpack/rspack configs, app.json, src/ structure, routes/, etc.

Explore the screens and component structure of this project to understand:

1. **Existing screens**: What screens exist under src/screens/? List them all.

5. **What does the src/modules/ folder contain?** (micro frontend module exports?)
6. **Entry points**: Look for index.ts/index.tsx at project root, App.tsx, any main export files.

Be thorough - check the actual file contents, not just file names.

content.content:
1→import { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  AirlineMilesSlice,
     5→  createAirlineMilesSlice,
     6→  createMarketplaceSlice,
     7→  createOrderDetailSlice,
     8→  createParameterPointsProgramSlice,
     9→  createPermissionSlice,
    10→  createPointsExtractSlice,
    11→  createPointsProductsSlice,
    12→  createRescueSlice,
    13→  MarketplaceSlice,
    14→  OrderDetailSlice,
    15→  ParameterPointsProgramSlice,
    16→  PermissionSlice,
    17→  PointsExtractSlice,
    18→  PointsProductsSlice,
    19→  RescueSlice,
    20→} from '../index';
    21→
    22→export type RewardsProgramRootStore = ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
    23→  PermissionSlice &
    24→  AirlineMilesSlice &
    25→  PointsExtractSlice &
    26→  PointsProductsSlice &
    27→  RescueSlice &
    28→  MarketplaceSlice &
    29→  OrderDetailSlice;
    30→
    31→type Slice<T> = StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>;
    32→const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>) =>
    33→  slice as Slice<T>;
    34→
    35→export function setRewardsProgramSlices<T extends RewardsProgramRootStore>(
    36→  ...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>
    37→) {
    38→  return {
    39→    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(
    40→      ...a,
    41→    ),
    42→    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    43→    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
    44→    ...asSlice<PointsExtractSlice>(createPointsExtractSlice)(...a),
    45→    ...asSlice<PointsProductsSlice>(createPointsProductsSlice)(...a),
    46→    ...asSlice<RescueSlice>(createRescueSlice)(...a),
    47→    ...asSlice<MarketplaceSlice>(createMarketplaceSlice)(...a),
    48→    ...asSlice<OrderDetailSlice>(createOrderDetailSlice)(...a),
    49→  };
    50→}

Explore this React Native project thoroughly to answer:

1. **Is this a micro frontend (MFE)?** Look for:
   - package.json (name, dependencies, re-pack/module-federation configs)
   - Any federation or re-pack config files (webpack, rspack, etc.)
   - How modules are exported (index.ts at root, App.tsx, etc.)
   - References to a "super app" or "host app"
   - Any `@semparar/` scoped packages in dependencies

2. **Where is the main/host app?** Look for:
   - References in package.json, README, or config files to a host/shell app
   - Navigation setup (routes/) that might reveal how this MFE is loaded
   - Any federation remote/host configuration

3. **Storybook feasibility:** Look for:
   - Any existing Storybook config (.storybook/ folder)
   - Existing stories files (*.stories.tsx)
   - The design system used (@semparar/design-system-app) and how components are imported
   - React Native version and compatibility considerations
   - Any ThemeProvider or context providers that screens depend on

1→import { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  AirlineMilesSlice,
     5→  createAirlineMilesSlice,
     6→  createMarketplaceSlice,
     7→  createOrderDetailSlice,
     8→  createParameterPointsProgramSlice,
     9→  createPermissionSlice,
    10→  createPointsExtractSlice,
    11→  createPointsProductsSlice,
    12→  createRescueSlice,
    13→  MarketplaceSlice,
    14→  OrderDetailSlice,
    15→  ParameterPointsProgramSlice,
    16→  PermissionSlice,
    17→  PointsExtractSlice,
    18→  PointsProductsSlice,
    19→  RescueSlice,
    20→} from '../index';
    21→
    22→export type RewardsProgramRootStore = ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
    23→  PermissionSlice &
    24→  AirlineMilesSlice &
    25→  PointsExtractSlice &
    26→  PointsProductsSlice &
    27→  RescueSlice &
    28→  MarketplaceSlice &
    29→  OrderDetailSlice;
    30→
    31→type Slice<T> = StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>;
    32→const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>) =>
    33→  slice as Slice<T>;
    34→
    35→export function setRewardsProgramSlices<T extends RewardsProgramRootStore>(
    36→  ...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>
    37→) {
    38→  return {
    39→    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(
    40→      ...a,
    41→    ),
    42→    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    43→    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
    44→    ...asSlice<PointsExtractSlice>(createPointsExtractSlice)(...a),
    45→    ...asSlice<PointsProductsSlice>(createPointsProductsSlice)(...a),
    46→    ...asSlice<RescueSlice>(createRescueSlice)(...a),
    47→    ...asSlice<MarketplaceSlice>(createMarketplaceSlice)(...a),
    48→    ...asSlice<OrderDetailSlice>(createOrderDetailSlice)(...a),
    49→  };
    50→}
    51→

content.content:
{
  "name": "AppSemPararV2",
  "version": "4.23.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "start": "react-native start",
    "test": "jest --maxWorkers=50% --passWithNoTests",
    "test:watch": "jest --watch --maxWorkers=25%",
    "test:ci": "jest --runInBand",
    "lint": "eslint --fix",
    "format": "yarn prettier --write --list-different '**/*.{ts,tsx}'",
    "bump-patch": "npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "bump-minor": "npm version minor --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "bump-major": "npm version major --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "postinstall": "npx jetify && yarn add_patch && yarn instrumentDynatrace && node ./scripts/mfUserFixed.js && patch-package",
    "build:ios": "react-native bundle --entry-file='index.js' --bundle-output='./ios/main.jsbundle' --dev=false --platform='ios'",
    "build:android:release": "cd android && ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew assembleRelease",
    "add_patch": "./scripts/add_patch.sh",
    "remove_patch": "./scripts/remove_patch.sh",
    "ios": "react-native run-ios",
    "android": "react-native run-android",
    "codegen": "react-native-codegen ./codegen.yaml",
    "instrumentDynatrace": "node node_modules/@dynatrace/react-native-plugin/scripts/instrument.js",
    "refreshVSToken": "vsts-npm-auth -config .npmrc",
    "prepare": "husky install",
    "prodrelease": "./scripts/prodrelease.sh",
    "search-ds-component": "./scripts/search_ds_component.sh",
    "setenv:dev": "cp .env.development .env && ENVFILE=.env.development && export ENVFILE=.env.development && echo \".env.development\" > /tmp/envfile",
    "setenv:qa": "cp .env.qa .env && ENVFILE=.env.qa && export ENVFILE=.env.qa && echo \".env.qa\" > /tmp/envfile",
    "setenv:prod": "cp .env.production .env && ENVFILE=.env.production && export ENVFILE=.env.production && echo \".env.production\" > /tmp/envfile"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dynatrace/react-native-plugin": "2.291.2",
    "@hookform/resolvers": "^2.9.10",
    "@noble/curves": "^2.0.1",
    "@noble/hashes": "^2.0.1",
    "@op-engineering/op-sqlite": "^14.0.1",
    "@react-native-async-storage/async-storage": "^2.1.2",
    "@react-native-clipboard/clipboard": "1.14.3",
    "@react-native-community/async-storage": "^1.12.1",
    "@react-native-community/blur": "^4.3.2",
    "@react-native-community/checkbox": "^0.5.16",
    "@react-native-community/geolocation": "^2.0.2",
    "@react-native-community/netinfo": "7.1.6",
    "@react-native-community/push-notification-ios": "^1.8.0",
    "@react-native-community/slider": "4.0.0-rc.3",
    "@react-native-community/toolbar-android": "^0.2.1",
    "@react-native-firebase/analytics": "^21.14.0",
    "@react-native-firebase/app": "^21.14.0",
    "@react-native-firebase/crashlytics": "^21.14.0",

content.text:
Perfect! I now have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings:

## Project Architecture Analysis: RNM App Fintech Reward Program

---

### 1. EXISTING SCREENS (src/screens/)

The project contains **12 main screens**:

1. **RewardProgramMarketplace** - WebView-based shopping marketplace
2. **AirlineMilesMarketplace** - WebView for airline miles redemption
3. **Points** - Main rewards points hub (balance, where to use points, my orders)
4. **LearnMoreRewardProgram** - Educational content about rewards
5. **PointsDetail** - Transaction detail view with reporting functionality
6. **ExtractRewardProgram** - Points transaction history with filters and search
7. **InvoiceCashbackOnboarding** - Invoice cashback setup flow
8. **InvoiceCashbackPoints** - Points selection for cashback
9. **InvoiceCashbackSummary** - Cashback transaction confirmation
10. **MyOrdersRewardProgram** - Order history/purchases made with points
11. **MyOrdersDetails** - Individual order details
12. (Implied) **Home/HomeScreen** - Entry point (referenced in navigation)

**Screen Pattern Structure:**
- Each screen is in its own folder: `src/screens/[ScreenName]/`
- Typically contains:
  - `index.ts` or `index.tsx` - Container component (handles logic, hooks, state)
  - `view.tsx` - Presentational component (UI only)
  - `Models/index.ts` or `models/index.ts` - TypeScript types/interfaces
  - `data/index.ts` (optional) - Constants/static data
  - `utils.ts` (optional) - Helper functions specific to screen

---

### 2. ATOMIC COMPONENTS STRUCTURE (src/atomic/)

**Three-level atomic design hierarchy:**

#### **Atoms** (`src/atomic/atoms/`)
- **AnimationArrows** - Loading animation component with models
- **Icons** - Icon library (referenced but minimal details)

#### **Organisms** (`src/atomic/organisms/`)
- **ApiError** - Error state handler with retry capability
  - Uses `useNavigation` to navigate to Home on error
  - Configurable title, subtitle, retry button
- **FaqContent** - FAQ section display
- **LearnMorePointsSection** - Educational content about points
- **PointsInfoBottomSheet** - Bottom modal showing points information
- **FiltersBottomSheet** - Filtration UI for transactions (period + types)

#### **Templates** (`src/atomic/templates/`)
- **BasicScreen** - Base screen wrapper with:
  - Status bar, header with left/right icons
  - Scrollable content area
  - Error state rendering
  - Loading overlay
  - Keyboard avoiding wrapper
  - Native back handler support
  
- **WebViewScreen** - WebView wrapper for marketplace screens
- **WrapperKeyboardAvoiding** - Keyboard avoid wrapper utility

**Component Patterns:**
- Container/Presenter split (index.tsx handles logic, view.tsx is UI)
- Model files export TypeScript interfaces
- All components use `@semparar/design-system-app` design tokens/components

---

### 3. ROUTING & NAVIGATION (src/routes/)

**File:** `/src/routes/index.tsx`

**Navigation Stack:** React Navigation's `createNativeStackNavigator`

**Route Configuration:**
```typescript
PropsFintechCardRoutes = {
  RewardProgramMarketplace,
  AirlineMilesMarketplace,
  RewardProgram (→ Points screen),
  LearnMoreRewardProgram,
  PointsDetail,
  ExtractRewardProgram,
  InvoiceCashbackOnboarding,
  InvoiceCashbackPoints,
  InvoiceCashbackSummary,
  MyOrdersRewardProgram,
  MyOrdersDetails
}
```

**Route Parameters:**
- Some routes accept `analytics` object (screen tracking data)
- `InvoiceCashbackOnboarding|Points|Summary`: `conversionFactor`, `productCode`, `campaingId`
- `PointsDetail`: requires `transaction` object (PointsTransaction type)
- `MyOrdersDetails`: requires `transactionId`, optional `type`, `icon`, `status`, `origination`

**Navigation Usage:**
- `useFocusEffect` from `@react-navigation/native` for screen-focused side effects
- `useNavigation` hook for programmatic navigation
- Analytics events fired on screen focus via `eventScreenViewV3`

---

### 4. REQUIRED PROVIDERS/WRAPPERS

**Root Store (Zustand):**
- `useRootStore` hook accesses global state
- Must be provided at app root via store configuration
- Import: `'~/store'` (path alias)

**Store Slices (Zustand):**
The store is composed of multiple slices:
1. `ParameterPointsProgramSlice` - Points program configuration
2. `PermissionSlice` - User permissions
3. `AirlineMilesSlice` - Airline miles data
4. `PointsExtractSlice` - Transaction history + filters
5. `PointsProductsSlice` - Redeemable products catalog
6. `RescueSlice` - Rescue/redemption operations
7. `MarketplaceSlice` - Marketplace data
8. `OrderDetailSlice` - Order details

**Theme Providers:**
- Design tokens from `@semparar/design-system-app`
- Status bar theming via `StatusBar` component
- Background colors via token system (e.g., `bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"`)

**Navigation Providers:**
- Standard React Navigation stack setup
- `<RewardsProgramRoutes />` exported as JSX element for composition
- Must be wrapped in `NavigationContainer` at parent level

**Analytics:**
- Global event functions: `eventScreenViewV3`, `eventSelectContent`, `eventModalView`

Perfect! I now have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings:

## Project Architecture Analysis: RNM App Fintech Reward Program

---

### 1. EXISTING SCREENS (src/screens/)

The project contains **12 main screens**:

1. **RewardProgramMarketplace** - WebView-based shopping marketplace
2. **AirlineMilesMarketplace** - WebView for airline miles redemption
3. **Points** - Main rewards points hub (balance, where to use points, my orders)
4. **LearnMoreRewardProgram** - Educational content about rewards
5. **PointsDetail** - Transaction detail view with reporting functionality
6. **ExtractRewardProgram** - Points transaction history with filters and search
7. **InvoiceCashbackOnboarding** - Invoice cashback setup flow
8. **InvoiceCashbackPoints** - Points selection for cashback
9. **InvoiceCashbackSummary** - Cashback transaction confirmation
10. **MyOrdersRewardProgram** - Order history/purchases made with points
11. **MyOrdersDetails** - Individual order details
12. (Implied) **Home/HomeScreen** - Entry point (referenced in navigation)

**Screen Pattern Structure:**
- Each screen is in its own folder: `src/screens/[ScreenName]/`
- Typically contains:
  - `index.ts` or `index.tsx` - Container component (handles logic, hooks, state)
  - `view.tsx` - Presentational component (UI only)
  - `Models/index.ts` or `models/index.ts` - TypeScript types/interfaces
  - `data/index.ts` (optional) - Constants/static data
  - `utils.ts` (optional) - Helper functions specific to screen

---

### 2. ATOMIC COMPONENTS STRUCTURE (src/atomic/)

**Three-level atomic design hierarchy:**

#### **Atoms** (`src/atomic/atoms/`)
- **AnimationArrows** - Loading animation component with models
- **Icons** - Icon library (referenced but minimal details)

#### **Organisms** (`src/atomic/organisms/`)
- **ApiError** - Error state handler with retry capability
  - Uses `useNavigation` to navigate to Home on error
  - Configurable title, subtitle, retry button
- **FaqContent** - FAQ section display
- **LearnMorePointsSection** - Educational content about points
- **PointsInfoBottomSheet** - Bottom modal showing points information
- **FiltersBottomSheet** - Filtration UI for transactions (period + types)

#### **Templates** (`src/atomic/templates/`)
- **BasicScreen** - Base screen wrapper with:
  - Status bar, header with left/right icons
  - Scrollable content area
  - Error state rendering
  - Loading overlay
  - Keyboard avoiding wrapper
  - Native back handler support
  
- **WebViewScreen** - WebView wrapper for marketplace screens
- **WrapperKeyboardAvoiding** - Keyboard avoid wrapper utility

**Component Patterns:**
- Container/Presenter split (index.tsx handles logic, view.tsx is UI)
- Model files export TypeScript interfaces
- All components use `@semparar/design-system-app` design tokens/components

---

### 3. ROUTING & NAVIGATION (src/routes/)

**File:** `/src/routes/index.tsx`

**Navigation Stack:** React Navigation's `createNativeStackNavigator`

**Route Configuration:**
```typescript
PropsFintechCardRoutes = {
  RewardProgramMarketplace,
  AirlineMilesMarketplace,
  RewardProgram (→ Points screen),
  LearnMoreRewardProgram,
  PointsDetail,
  ExtractRewardProgram,
  InvoiceCashbackOnboarding,
  InvoiceCashbackPoints,
  InvoiceCashbackSummary,
  MyOrdersRewardProgram,
  MyOrdersDetails
}
```

**Route Parameters:**
- Some routes accept `analytics` object (screen tracking data)
- `InvoiceCashbackOnboarding|Points|Summary`: `conversionFactor`, `productCode`, `campaingId`
- `PointsDetail`: requires `transaction` object (PointsTransaction type)
- `MyOrdersDetails`: requires `transactionId`, optional `type`, `icon`, `status`, `origination`

**Navigation Usage:**
- `useFocusEffect` from `@react-navigation/native` for screen-focused side effects
- `useNavigation` hook for programmatic navigation
- Analytics events fired on screen focus via `eventScreenViewV3`

---

### 4. REQUIRED PROVIDERS/WRAPPERS

**Root Store (Zustand):**
- `useRootStore` hook accesses global state
- Must be provided at app root via store configuration
- Import: `'~/store'` (path alias)

**Store Slices (Zustand):**
The store is composed of multiple slices:
1. `ParameterPointsProgramSlice` - Points program configuration
2. `PermissionSlice` - User permissions
3. `AirlineMilesSlice` - Airline miles data
4. `PointsExtractSlice` - Transaction history + filters
5. `PointsProductsSlice` - Redeemable products catalog
6. `RescueSlice` - Rescue/redemption operations
7. `MarketplaceSlice` - Marketplace data
8. `OrderDetailSlice` - Order details

**Theme Providers:**
- Design tokens from `@semparar/design-system-app`
- Status bar theming via `StatusBar` component
- Background colors via token system (e.g., `bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"`)

**Navigation Providers:**
- Standard React Navigation stack setup
- `<RewardsProgramRoutes />` exported as JSX element for composition
- Must be wrapped in `NavigationContainer` at parent level

**Analytics:**
- Global event functions: `eventScreenViewV3`, `eventSelectContent`, `eventModalView`
- Imported from `'~/constants/functions'` (path alias)

---

### 5. SERVICES/MODULES STRUCTURE (src/services/)

**Layered Architecture:**

```
src/services/
├── api/                     # API client calls
│   ├── airlineMiles.api.ts
│   ├── marketplace.api.ts
│   ├── orderDetail.api.ts
│   ├── parameter.api.ts
│   ├── permission.api.ts
│   ├── products.api.ts
│   ├── rescue.api.ts
│   └── extract.api.ts
├── models/                  # TypeScript types/interfaces
│   ├── airlineMiles.model.ts
│   ├── marketplace.model.ts
│   ├── extract.model.ts
│   └── ... (one per domain)
├── repository/              # Data layer abstraction
│   ├── extract.repository.ts
│   ├── parameter.repository.ts
│   ├── permission.repository.ts
│   └── ... (one per domain)
├── services/                # Business logic
│   ├── airlineMiles.service.ts
│   ├── extract.service.ts
│   ├── marketplace.service.ts
│   └── ... (one per domain)
├── store/                   # Zustand store slices
│   ├── airlineMiles.store.ts
│   ├── extract.store.ts
│   ├── products.store.ts
│   ├── slices.ts            # Root store composition
│   └── ... (one per domain)
├── hooks/                   # React hooks for screens
│   ├── usePoints.ts
│   ├── useAirlineMiles.ts
│   ├── useMarketplace.ts
│   ├── useRescue.ts
│   ├── usePermission.ts
│   └── index.ts (exports all)
├── utils/
│   ├── cleanup.ts
│   └── participantId.ts
├── constants.ts
└── index.ts (exports everything)
```

**Data Flow:**
1. **API** → Calls backend endpoints
2. **Repository** → Abstracts API calls
3. **Service** → Business logic + store mutations
4. **Store** (Zustand) → Global state management
5. **Hooks** → React hooks consuming store via `useRootStore`
6. **Screens** → Consume hooks for data/actions

**Key Service Methods:**
- `getPointsBalance()`, `getPointsProducts()`, `getPointsExtract()`
- `getParameterPointsProgram()`
- `getPointsExtract()` - Supports pagination (page parameter)
- All services handle loading states via store slices

---

### 6. ENTRY POINTS

**Primary Entry (Package.json):**
```json
"main": "./src/index.ts"
```

**Export File:** `/src/index.ts`
```typescript

```

**Integration Points:**

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

1. **Container/Presenter Pattern**: Logic separated from UI
2. **Custom Hooks**: Heavy use of `useRootStore` with `useShallow` for optimized subscriptions
3. **Micro-frontend Module**: Exports routes for parent app composition
4. **Zustand Store**: Centralized state with slice-based architecture

6. **Design System**: All UI components from `@semparar/design-system-app`

---

### CRITICAL DEPENDENCIES

### 2. **Host/Super App Location: `../appSemPararv3`**

  ```typescript
  import { setRewardsProgramSlices, type RewardsProgramRootStore } from '@semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram';

  export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(...);
  ```

- **Git Remote**: No federation-specific config files; integration via npm/yalc packages

---

### 3. **Storybook Feasibility: FEASIBLE BUT WITH CAVEATS**

**Current Status:**
- **No Storybook setup** in this MFE (no `.storybook/` folder, no `*.stories.tsx` files)
- **No Storybook dependencies** in package.json

**Positive Factors for Feasibility:**

- **Design System with Storybook**: `@semparar/design-system-app` has full Storybook setup:

  - No hard dependencies on navigation at atom/molecule level
  - Screens separated in `src/screens/`

**Critical Blockers & Challenges:**

1. **Zustand Store Dependency**: Components use `useRootStore` hook directly
   - Example from `src/services/hooks/useRescue.ts`: `const rescue = useRootStore(...)`
   - Components need store mocking or provider wrapping

2. **Navigation Stack Dependency**: Routes export a React Navigation Native Stack
   - Screens depend on `navigation.navigate()` calls
   - Example from `src/screens/Points/index.tsx`: `navigation.navigate('MyOrdersRewardProgram')`

4. **ThemeProvider/Context**: Components import styled components from design system
   - May require theme context to be available
   - Design system likely uses styled-components v6.1.14

---

### **Recommended Storybook Setup Approach:**

For this MFE to have Storybook stories, you would need to:

1. **Create wrapper components** that provide required context:
   - Zustand store with default/mock state
   - Navigation props mock
   - Design system theme provider

2. **Structure stories to test atomic level**:

4. **Configuration template**: The design system's `.storybook/main.ts` shows the React Native Web integration pattern that would work here

---

### **Project Structure Summary**

```
src/
├── index.ts (MFE exports: routes, store, types, utils)

```

  - Atomic design pattern (`src/atomic/atoms`, `organisms`, `templates`)
  - Independent, reusable components
  - No hard dependencies on navigation at atom/molecule level
  - Screens separated in `src/screens/`

**Critical Blockers & Challenges:**

1. **Zustand Store Dependency**: Components use `useRootStore` hook directly
   - Example from `src/services/hooks/useRescue.ts`: `const rescue = useRootStore(...)`
   - Components need store mocking or provider wrapping
   - Store is created in the host app, not in this MFE

2. **Navigation Stack Dependency**: Routes export a React Navigation Native Stack
   - Screens depend on `navigation.navigate()` calls
   - Example from `src/screens/Points/index.tsx`: `navigation.navigate('MyOrdersRewardProgram')`

3. **Design System Components**: Heavy use of `@semparar/design-system-app` components
   - Requires the design system package to be installed
   - May have internal theme/provider dependencies

4. **ThemeProvider/Context**: Components import styled components from design system
   - May require theme context to be available
   - Design system likely uses styled-components v6.1.14

---

### **Recommended Storybook Setup Approach:**

For this MFE to have Storybook stories, you would need to:

1. **Create wrapper components** that provide required context:
   - Zustand store with default/mock state
   - Navigation props mock
   - Design system theme provider

2. **Structure stories to test atomic level**:
   - Focus on `src/atomic/atoms/**` and `src/atomic/organisms/**` for easiest isolation
   - Create decorator functions for common providers
   - Mock service hooks at the boundary

3. **Reference the design system's Storybook**:
   - Located at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/design-system-app/.storybook`
   - Uses same Storybook 8.3.3 + React configuration
   - Can reuse decorator patterns from design system stories

4. **Configuration template**: The design system's `.storybook/main.ts` shows the React Native Web integration pattern that would work here

---

### **Project Structure Summary**

```
src/
├── index.ts (MFE exports: routes, store, types, utils)
├── routes/index.tsx (RewardsProgramRoutes - Stack navigator)
├── screens/ (12 screens, all depend on navigation)
├── atomic/
│   ├── atoms/ (basic components)
│   ├── organisms/ (complex components - uses useRootStore)
│   └── templates/ (BasicScreen, WebViewScreen - uses @semparar/design-system-app)
├── services/
│   ├── store/ (Zustand slices - 8 slice files)
│   ├── hooks/ (usePoints, useRescue, etc. - depend on useRootStore)
│   ├── api/ (11 API service files)
│   └── repository/ (data transformation)
└── utils/ (helpers)
```

**State Management**: Zustand with 8 slices, exported as composable `setRewardsProgramSlices()` function for host app integration

Now I have comprehensive information about the project. Let me summarize the answers to the user's three questions.

1. **Is this a micro frontend?** Yes, it's `@semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram`
2. **Where is the main app?** `../appSemPararv3` (SuperApp)
3. **Can we configure Storybook?** Feasible but with caveats

Let me provide a clear, organized answer.

content.text:
Here are the answers:

## 1. Sim, é um Micro Frontend (MFE)

The package name is `@semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram`. The entry point (`src/index.ts`) exports composable pieces for the host app:

```ts
export { RewardsProgramRoutes } from './routes';
export { type RewardsProgramRootStore } from './services';
export { setRewardsProgramSlices } from './services/store/slices';
export { clearRewardsProgramStore } from './services/utils';
```

It's consumed by the host via npm/yalc — no Module Federation or Re.Pack involved.

## 2. O app principal fica em `../appSemPararv3`

The README documents it explicitly: *"Este pacote é um micro-frontend consumido pelo SuperApp"*. The host app is `AppSemPararV2` (v4.23.0) at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/appSemPararv3`. It integrates this MFE's Zustand slices into its root store and renders `RewardsProgramRoutes` inside its navigation stack.

## 3. Storybook — Viável, com ressalvas

**Good news:**
- The design system (`@semparar/design-system-app`) already has Storybook 8.3.3 configured with `@storybook/addon-react-native-web` — so the pattern exists in the ecosystem
- React 18.2.0 is fully compatible
- The Hook Component pattern (logic hook + dumb view) is ideal for Storybook — views are easy to isolate

**Challenges to solve:**
- **Zustand store** — screens use `useRootStore` which is created in the host app. Needs a mock store wrapper.
- **Navigation** — screens depend on `useNavigation`/`useFocusEffect`. Needs a mock `NavigationContainer`.
- **Design system** — components from `@semparar/design-system-app` may need a ThemeProvider.
- **Path aliases** — `~/store`, `~/constants/functions` etc. resolve to the host app. Need webpack aliases in Storybook config.

**What would work well in Storybook:**
- `src/atomic/atoms/*` — easiest, minimal dependencies
- `src/atomic/organisms/*` — moderate, some need store mocking
- `src/atomic/templates/*` — `BasicScreen`, `WebViewScreen`
- Screen `view.tsx` files — since they're pure render components, they can receive props directly without the hook

content.content:
1→# Fintech Rewards Program
     2→
     3→Este micro-frontend é um da vertical de serviços financeiros do SuperApp do Sem Parar.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Scripts NPM
     8→
     9→- `test` - executa Jest
    10→- `test:update-snapshot` - atualiza snapshots do Jest
    11→- `test:coverage` - executa Jest com cobertura
    12→- `sync:yalc` - publica com `yalc` e faz push em mudanças para desenvolvimento integrado com o `../superAppV3`
    13→- `knip` - knip busca código e dependências sem uso ou faltando, imports errados, entre outros - [saiba mais](https://knip.dev/).
    14→- `lint` - executa checagens e formatação do `eslint`, `prettier` e `typescript`.
    15→   O comando `run-s` utilizado pelo script `lint` faz parte do [npm-run-all](https://www.npmjs.com/package/npm-run-all)
    16→
    17→## Pré-requisitos
    18→
    19→Instale as ferramentas abaixo nas versões recomendadas para garantir compatibilidade:
    20→
    21→| Ferramenta         | Versão                        | Como instalar                                            |
    22→| :----------------- | :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------- |
    23→| OpenJDK            | 17                            | `brew install --cask zulu@17`                            |
    24→| Node.js            | LTS                           | Recomendado via NVM                                      |
    25→| Ruby               | 2.7.4                         | Use rbenv/rvm                                            |
    26→| CocoaPods          | >= 1.11.2                     | `gem install cocoapods -v "~> 1.11"` ou `bundle install` |
    27→| Xcode              | Compatível com a versão do RN | Veja versões disponíveis em https://xcodereleases.com    |
    28→| Android Studio/SDK | Atual                         | Siga guia oficial do RN                                  |
    29→
    30→Notas:
    31→
    32→1. Em caso de problemas com Xcode, baixe a versão recomendada em [xcodereleases.com](https://xcodereleases.com/).
    33→2. Siga o guia oficial de ambiente iOS/Android: https://reactnative.dev/docs/set-up-your-environment
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Acesso e configuração
    38→
    39→Importante: é necessário estar conectado à VPN e logado com o usuário GitHub do Sem Parar para acesso aos repositórios.
    40→
    41→### 1) Clonar o repositório
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→git clone https://github.com/SemParar-B2C/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram.git
    45→cd rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram
    46→```
    47→
    48→### 2) Instalar dependências
    49→
    50→```bash
    51→# Node deps
    52→npm install
    53→```
    54→
    55→### 3) SuperApp (integração local)
    56→
    57→Este pacote é um micro-frontend consumido pelo SuperApp. Para desenvolver integrado:
    58→
    59→1. Tenha o SuperApp clonado como diretório `../appSemPararv3`
    60→2. Sincronize este pacote com o SuperApp:
    61→   - `npm run sync:yalc`
    62→   - Requer `yalc` e `nodemon`; publica no yalc e faz push on change.
    63→
    64→---
    65→
    66→## Consumo de código do SuperApp
    67→
    68→Este pacote é um micro-frontend usado pelo SuperApp, e aqui também importamos código do SuperApp por meio de aliases.
    69→Todo import cujo path inicia com `~/` é resolvido para o código do próprio SuperApp.
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Testes
    74→
    75→- Framework: Jest (`jest.config.js`)
    76→- Bibliotecas: `@testing-library/react-native`, `@testing-library/jest-native`
    77→- Ambiente: `testEnvironment: 'node'` com mocks RN
    78→- Paths de testes: `__tests__/**/*.test.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}`
    79→- Cobertura: saída em `__tests__/coverage` (relatórios: text, lcov, html)
    80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→## Links importantes
    84→
    85→- SharePoint Fintech: https://fleetcor.sharepoint.com/sites/SemPararMais-Fintech
    86→

content.content:
1→import { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  AirlineMilesSlice,
     5→  createAirlineMilesSlice,
     6→  createMarketplaceSlice,
     7→  createOrderDetailSlice,
     8→  createParameterPointsProgramSlice,
     9→  createPermissionSlice,
    10→  createPointsExtractSlice,
    11→  createPointsProductsSlice,
    12→  createRescueSlice,
    13→  MarketplaceSlice,
    14→  OrderDetailSlice,
    15→  ParameterPointsProgramSlice,
    16→  PermissionSlice,
    17→  PointsExtractSlice,
    18→  PointsProductsSlice,
    19→  RescueSlice,
    20→} from '../index';
    21→
    22→export type RewardsProgramRootStore = ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
    23→  PermissionSlice &
    24→  AirlineMilesSlice &
    25→  PointsExtractSlice &
    26→  PointsProductsSlice &
    27→  RescueSlice &
    28→  MarketplaceSlice &
    29→  OrderDetailSlice;
    30→
    31→type Slice<T> = StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>;
    32→const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>) =>
    33→  slice as Slice<T>;
    34→
    35→export function setRewardsProgramSlices<T extends RewardsProgramRootStore>(
    36→  ...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>
    37→) {
    38→  return {
    39→    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(
    40→      ...a,
    41→    ),
    42→    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    43→    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
    44→    ...asSlice<PointsExtractSlice>(createPointsExtractSlice)(...a),
    45→    ...asSlice<PointsProductsSlice>(createPointsProductsSlice)(...a),
    46→    ...asSlice<RescueSlice>(createRescueSlice)(...a),
    47→    ...asSlice<MarketplaceSlice>(createMarketplaceSlice)(...a),
    48→    ...asSlice<OrderDetailSlice>(createOrderDetailSlice)(...a),
    49→  };
    50→}
    51→

content.thinking:
The user is making an excellent point. They're asking me to think critically about what actually saves tokens and provides value in a CLAUDE.md file. Let me think about this properly:

**What wastes tokens in a new session:**
1. Claude having to explore and discover that this is an MFE, how it's consumed, where the host app is — this is non-obvious and expensive to rediscover
2. Understanding the architectural patterns (Hook Component, Zustand-only, service layers) — Claude would need to read STYLE_GUIDE.md every time
3. Understanding how the store integration works (slices exported to host app) — this is unique to this project
4. Path aliases and how they resolve — `~/` resolves to host app paths, which is confusing


5. The SDUI/parameterization RFC context — ongoing architectural decision

**What does NOT belong in CLAUDE.md:**
- Package.json scripts (Claude can just read package.json, it's cheap)
- File listing (Claude can glob)
- Obvious stuff like "this uses React Native" (visible from any file)
- Duplicating STYLE_GUIDE.md content (it's already in `.claude/`)

So the CLAUDE.md should focus on the non-obvious, expensive-to-discover knowledge that would otherwise require many tool calls to figure out in a fresh session — like the MFE consumption model, the unique store integration pattern, path alias resolution, and ongoing architectural decisions.

content.text:
You're absolutely right. The value of CLAUDE.md is in the **non-obvious, expensive-to-rediscover knowledge** — things that would cost 20+ tool calls to figure out in a fresh session. Not the trivial stuff Claude can read from `package.json` in one call.

Here's my reasoning on what actually matters:

**High-value (saves many tokens to rediscover):**
- This is an MFE, the host app is at `../appSemPararv3`, integration is via yalc
- `useRootStore` is created in the **host app**, not here — this MFE exports slices via `setRewardsProgramSlices`
- Path aliases like `~/store`, `~/constants` resolve to the **host app**, not this project — this is extremely confusing for Claude without context
- `.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md` is the authoritative pattern guide (so Claude doesn't reinvent conventions)
- `.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt` is the SDUI RFC (ongoing architectural direction)
- The design system is `@semparar/design-system-app` and lives at `../design-system-app`

**Low-value (cheap to discover, don't bother):**
- Scripts in package.json
- File/folder listing
- "This uses React Native" / "This uses TypeScript"
- Dependencies list
- Linting/prettier rules

**Borderline (useful but short):**
- The naming convention for routes (`SPPayment`, `FTHPayment`)
- The sync workflow (`yalc push` → SuperApp)

So the CLAUDE.md should be a **compact architectural cheat sheet** — maybe 40-60 lines — that eliminates the most expensive exploration loops.

Now I have all the details I need. Let me draft the CLAUDE.md and write it to the plan file for review.

The key insight from `@types/store.d.ts` is crucial — it uses TypeScript module augmentation to declare `~/store` as a module that exports `useRootStore`. This is the mechanism by which the MFE can `import { useRootStore } from '~/store'` and have it resolve to the host app's store at runtime. This is non-obvious and would be very confusing for Claude without context.

Let me now draft the CLAUDE.md and put it in the plan file.

content.text: Now I have everything. The `@types/store.d.ts` confirms the critical non-obvious pattern — `~/store` is a **module augmentation** that resolves to the SuperApp's store at runtime. This is exactly the kind of thing that would confuse Claude without context.

input.content:
# Plan: Populate project-level CLAUDE.md

## Context

The project-level `.claude/CLAUDE.md` is empty. This file is loaded into every Claude Code session as system context. It should contain **non-obvious, expensive-to-rediscover knowledge** that saves dozens of exploration tool calls in fresh sessions.

## Proposed CLAUDE.md content

```markdown
# @semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram

Micro frontend for the Sem Parar Mais rewards program (points balance, redemptions, vouchers, cashback, marketplace).

## Architecture — MFE ↔ SuperApp

This is NOT a standalone app. It's an MFE package consumed by the SuperApp (`AppSemPararV2`) at `../appSemPararv3` via yalc.

**Exports** (`src/index.ts`):
- `RewardsProgramRoutes` — `<Stack.Screen>` fragments injected into the SuperApp's navigator
- `setRewardsProgramSlices` — Zustand slice factory composed into the SuperApp's root store
- `RewardsProgramRootStore` — type union of all 8 slices
- `clearRewardsProgramStore` — cleanup utility

**Critical: `~/store` is the SuperApp's store, not ours.**
`@types/store.d.ts` uses TS module augmentation to declare `useRootStore` from `~/store`. At runtime, it resolves to the host app's Zustand store. We export slices; the host composes them. Never create a local `useRootStore` — use the augmented import.

## Patterns & Conventions

Authoritative guide: `.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md` — read it before writing any component or service.

Key points:
- **Hook Component pattern**: `index.ts` (logic hook) + `view.tsx` (render) + `Models/index.d.ts` (types)

- **Design system**: `@semparar/design-system-app` — no StyleSheet, no styled-components
- **Selectors**: `useRootStore(s => s.slice.field)` with `useShallow` for multi-field selects

`.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt` describes the Server Driven UI proposal for parameterizable screens. The voucher screens (SPMAIS-34653) are the first implementation target. Backend sends JSON with `header`, `body[]`, `footer`; app renders via component registry. Uses `{{var}}` interpolation and JSON Logic rules for conditionals.

## Store Slices (8 total)

`ParameterPointsProgram` · `Permission` · `AirlineMiles` · `PointsExtract` · `PointsProducts` · `Rescue` · `Marketplace` · `OrderDetail`
```

The draft is in the plan file. Key design decisions:

- **~50 lines** — compact enough to not bloat context, dense enough to eliminate the costliest rediscovery loops
- **Focuses on the #1 confusion point**: `~/store` module augmentation — this would trip up Claude every single session without context

- **Omits** everything cheap to discover (scripts, deps, file listings, linting rules)
